reasoning: Beto O'Rourke recently indicated a very strong likelihood of a 2026 Senate run, stating in April 2025: 'If... this is what the people of Texas want... then yes, I will.' He is currently conducting a series of 'testing the waters' town halls scheduled throughout May. Historically, Texas Democratic challengers for the Senate have announced their runs in the spring of the off-year (e.g., Allred in May, Hegar in April, O'Rourke himself in March 2017). With his current tour wrapping up in late May, a June announcement would align perfectly with historical precedents. Additionally, announcing in mid-June allows him to capitalize on the end-of-quarter FEC deadline (June 30) by leveraging urgency for initial fundraising. However, because he is a well-known figure who previously delayed a campaign start (announcing for governor in Nov 2021), and because a launch exactly on July 1 to kick off Q3 funding cannot be ruled out, the probability is kept somewhat moderate. Overall, I estimate his chances of running at around 85%, and if he runs, a ~73% chance that the announcement comes before the July 1 deadline.
batch_queries: Will Beto O'Rourke announce his candidacy for US Senator from Texas before July 1, 2025?
